data.narrative:
SatRev is a Series B–stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Zwolinski, Damian Fijalkowski, and Radoslaw Lapczyński. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private peers and 6 publicly traded Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Three Series B Space & Aerospace companies—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M)—yielded a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 42.9x median price-to-sales multiple cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, centered on $1.3B. This reflects the peer-median benchmark with modest adjustability for stage and market positioning. Key uncertainties include the absence of target revenue disclosure (which prevents revenue-multiple cross-check), undisclosed prior fundraising history, and the wide dispersion of private peer valuations ($400M to $2.0B), which constrains confidence in the central estimate. The tightness of the applicable method set—relying on a single triangulation approach—represents a material limitation to valuation precision.
